strategic_objective: Elucidate how feature redundancies interact with class-conditioned structural variations to affect signal clarity and stability.
key_checks: Analyze dependency structures linking redundant features with class-conditioned packet length and size variations
Evaluate if disentangling redundancies clarifies or amplifies meaningful class-specific signals
Check for unstable dependencies that may mask or distort class-conditioned variation
Assess impact of redundancy disentanglement on interpretability and detection of class patterns
success_criteria: Improved understanding of how redundancy modulates class-conditioned feature variation
Identification of strategies to separate stable class signals from redundant noise
Evidence that disentangling dependencies enhances interpretability and robustness of class-specific patterns
router_constraints: preserve feature scope on packet length and size features
preserve evidence refs related to redundancy and class variation (e9 to e22)
